payload.answer: recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman because it was identified as the newer/superseding memory for the same Sam+Goldman context. mem_old was filtered out since it was marked as superseded by mem_new.
payload.conflict_classification: Supersession/recency conflict (same entity-context pair represented by multiple memory cards).
payload.decision: Keep mem_new; filter out mem_old because it is superseded by the newer, higher-priority record for the same entity/attribute set (Sam + Goldman).

entity_resolution.reason: Both mem_new and mem_old resolve to the same intended referent (Sam associated with Goldman). The system applied a supersession rule, preferring the newer record and excluding the older one.

payload.intent: Explain conflict resolution in recall results for Sam from Goldman, where mem_new is returned and mem_old is filtered as superseded.

memory_cards.statement: recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman.
recall filtered mem_old because it was superseded.

details: Applied supersession rule for overlapping memory claims about the same entity/context (Sam + Goldman).
Kept the higher-priority record (mem_new) and filtered the older one (mem_old) as superseded.

repair_options: If mem_old should not be considered superseded, update tagging/versioning or disable supersession for that specific memory source.
If both memories are valid, revise mem_new or mem_old so they do not overlap in the conflict key (e.g., separate by different attributes/time scopes).
Ensure entity-linking rules correctly treat mem_old as distinct (e.g., disambiguate Sam identities or differentiate Goldman-related attributes).
